title: Brewers reportedly agree to deal with veteran infielder Luis Rengifo
description: The Brewers appear to have solved their third-base problem for now by agreeing to a one-year deal with veteran utility man Luis Rengifo.
fullText:
PHOENIX – Just like that, it appears the Milwaukee Brewers have their third baseman for 2026.

According to a report from MLB.com's Mark Feinsand, Luis Rengifo has agreed to terms on a one-year pact worth $3.5 million with another $1.5 million available in incentives.

The deal, according to Feinsand, also includes a $10 million mutual option for 2027.

As of early Friday evening, Feb. 13 the Brewers had yet to announce the transaction.

To see Milwaukee make a move for Rengifo is hardly surprising considering the position had been thrown into tumult since the surprise trade of last season's starter, Caleb Durbin, to the Boston Red Sox on Feb. 9.

David Hamilton, acquired in the Durbin deal, and Jett Williams were the most likely candidates for the job heading into camp until the Rengifo addition, with prospects Brock Wilken, Luke Adams and Jesús Made also expected to see time at third.

In Rengifo, the Brewers are buying low on a seven-year veteran of the major leagues who is coming off a down 2025 after compiling three straight productive seasons for the Los Angeles Angels.

A switch-hitter who turns 29 on Feb. 26, Rengifo hit .238 with nine home runs and 43 runs batted in with an OPS of .622 and 10 stolen bases in a career-high 147 games with the Angels last year.

Rengifo's strikeout rate isn't awful (19.2%, 62nd percentile in the majors) but he doesn't walk much, either (6.1%, 24th percentile). In addition, he struggled hitting the ball hard (24th percentile or lower in three key categories) with just 15th percentile bat speed.

Defensively, Rengifo also struggled, accounting for minus-seven outs above average and minus-five defensive runs saved in 76 games at third with a below-average throwing arm.

One facet of Rengifo's game is right up the Brewers' alley – versatility. He also played 74 games at second base and 10 more spread between all three outfield positions.

Rengifo even logged a couple innings at shortstop.

He represents the second veteran to reportedly agree to terms with the Brewers this week; catcher Gary Sánchez is also expected to come aboard on a one-year deal to serve as backup to William Contreras.

This article originally appeared on Milwaukee Journal Sentinel: Brewers reportedly agree to deal with veteran infielder Luis Rengifo

title: Pinnington Jones' dream Dallas run ended by Cilic
description: Great Britain's Jack Pinnington Jones has his dream run at the Dallas Open ended by veteran Marin Cilic in the quarter-finals.
fullText:
Briton Jack Pinnington Jones had his dream run at the Dallas Open ended by veteran Marin Cilic in the quarter-finals.

The 22-year-old progressed through two rounds of qualifying to make the tournament's main draw, before upsetting Italy's world number 20 Flavio Cobolli and 68th-ranked American Eliot Spizzirri to reach the last eight.

But, contesting the first ATP Tour quarter-final of his career, Pinnington Jones was beaten by 2014 US Open champion Cilic 6-1 6-4 in just 78 minutes.

The former world number three, 37, will meet the winner of the all-American match between top seed Taylor Fritz and Sebastian Korda for a place in the final.

At the Qatar Open, Canadian teenager Victoria Mboko followed up her shock win over Australian Open champion Elena Rybakina by defeating former French Open champion Jelena Ostapenko to reach the final.

The 19-year-old prevailed 6-3 6-2 to set up a title decider against Karolina Muchova, after the Czech 14th seed came from behind to defeat Maria Sakkari 3-6 6-4 6-1 - a day after the Greek player knocked out top seed Iga Swiatek.

Pinnington Jones is set to rise 43 places in the ATP rankings, up to 138 in the world from 181st, following his memorable week.

He has embarked on a strong run of form since losing his first qualifying match at the Australian Open in January, reaching the final at the Soma Bay challenger event in Egypt prior to an impressive four-match winning run in Dallas.

That included achieving the biggest win of his career by overcoming Cobolli in his opening match.

It came after Pinnington Jones opted against returning for his final year at Texas Christian University, having completed his junior season last May in order to pursue a full-time tennis career.

After holding his opening service game, Pinnington Jones conceded a fourth break point in his second as Cilic put together a run of five straight games to seal a one-sided first set.

The Croat then broke his opponent's serve in the fifth game of the second set on his way to victory - although he was made to withstand two break points as he served for the match.
